quote: Satō ex. (oral-literature register). The bare concept form hum (not possessed humi) appears before konna. Satō classifies konna/kan provisionally as a 強調の助詞 (emphatic 副助詞) but flags its grammatical status as 'unclear'; the appearance of the concept form rather than the possessed form before konna is unexplained — the same nouns take possessed forms when functioning as formal-noun evidential heads. See formal-defective-nouns for the possessed/concept-form alternation.
